sources.name: Per-NSIP project-history research
sources.note: Project-history milestones for each NSIP, gathered by a Claude research agent (or hand-curated for seed entries) from public sources: Wikipedia, BAILII (judicial reviews), national press, applicant disclosures and ministerial announcements. Surfaces the pre-DCO history that PINS doesn't track — earlier proposal cycles, public inquiries, dormant decades, judicial reviews, route changes — so the dashboard can show the full project arc rather than just the modern consenting window.

timelines.description: Per-NSIP project history keyed by NSIP reference. Each entry has a milestones array with date / phase / title / description / source.

TR010025.name: A303 Stonehenge
TR010025.summary: Proposed in some form since the early 1990s. Two cancellations, a Labour-government scrapping in 2007, a 2014 revival, a 2020 DCO grant, a 2021 High Court quashing on heritage grounds, a 2023 re-issue, and a final 2024 cancellation by the incoming Labour government on cost grounds. Roughly 35 years of proposals for ~2 km of tunnel.

milestones.title: A303 trunk-route priority
milestones.description: Government identifies A303 as priority south-west corridor; tunnel options floated.

milestones.title: First tunnel proposal
milestones.description: Highways Agency sets out short-bore tunnel option past Stonehenge.

milestones.title: Public inquiry opens
milestones.description: Years of inquiry into competing surface and tunnel options.

milestones.title: Scheme cancelled (cost)
milestones.description: Labour government drops scheme citing unaffordable £540m bill.

milestones.title: Revived under Road Investment Strategy
milestones.description: Highways England commits to a longer 2.9 km tunnel as part of RIS1.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation rounds
milestones.description: Multiple consultations on alignment, portal location, heritage mitigation.

milestones.title: DCO submitted (first)
milestones.description: Highways England submits DCO; later withdrawn for revision.

milestones.title: DCO granted
milestones.description: Grant Shapps approves against Examining Authority's recommendation to refuse.

milestones.title: High Court quashes consent
milestones.description: Save Stonehenge WHS challenge succeeds — Mr Justice Holgate finds heritage assessment unlawful.

milestones.title: DCO re-issued
milestones.description: Mark Harper reapproves the same scheme after fresh assessment.

milestones.title: Second JR fails
milestones.description: Court of Appeal upholds the re-issued DCO.

milestones.title: Cancelled (Labour, cost)
milestones.description: New Labour government scraps the £2.5bn scheme alongside other RIS pipeline projects.

EN010012.name: The Sizewell C Project
EN010012.summary: Sizewell C is a 3.2 GW twin-EPR nuclear project in Suffolk first proposed by EDF in 2012 following the UK's 2008 new-nuclear policy. After four rounds of pre-application consultation (2012-2019), DCO was submitted in May 2020 and granted in July 2022; following judicial review challenges that were ultimately rejected by the Supreme Court in May 2024, the project reached Final Investment Decision in July 2025 at a £38 billion capital cost (2024 prices), roughly double the £20 billion 2020 baseline.

milestones.title: UK New Nuclear White Paper
milestones.description: Government policy opens door to new nuclear on existing sites.

milestones.title: Sizewell nominated as new-nuclear site
milestones.description: Sizewell named among eight sites suitable for new nuclear development by 2025.

milestones.title: Stage 1 pre-application consultation
milestones.description: EDF launches first public consultation on two EPR reactors at Sizewell C.

milestones.title: Stage 2 consultation
milestones.description: Second statutory consultation runs into early 2017.

milestones.title: Stage 3 consultation
milestones.description: Third pre-application consultation runs Jan-March 2019.

milestones.title: Stage 4 consultation
milestones.description: Final pre-application consultation runs Jul-Sep 2019.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: EDF/NNB submits Development Consent Order at projected £20bn cost.

milestones.title: PINS examination opens
milestones.description: Six-month examination runs to October 2021.

milestones.title: Nuclear Energy (Financing) Act passes
milestones.description: Enables Regulated Asset Base (RAB) financing model for nuclear, designed to support Sizewell C.

milestones.title: DCO granted by Secretary of State
milestones.description: Kwasi Kwarteng grants development consent against the Examining Authority's advice.

milestones.title: Together Against Sizewell C files JR
milestones.description: TASC challenges DCO grant on water supply and policy grounds.

milestones.title: UK government buys out CGN, takes 50% stake
milestones.description: Treasury commits £679m to remove China General Nuclear, partner with EDF on national security grounds.

milestones.title: High Court dismisses JR
milestones.description: Mr Justice Holgate rejects all grounds, calling some 'totally without merit'.

milestones.title: Site preparation begins
milestones.description: Early enabling works commence at the Suffolk site ahead of FID.

milestones.title: Supreme Court refuses JR appeal
milestones.description: Final determination of legal challenge; DCO confirmed.

milestones.title: Final Investment Decision at £38bn
milestones.description: UK government 44.9% lead stake; La Caisse 20%, Centrica 15%, Amber 7.6%, EDF 12.5%. First power targeted late 2030s.

milestones.title: First reactor target (originally)
milestones.description: Original first-power target at 2020 baseline. EDF's 2024 update slipped this to ~2031.

milestones.title: First power (current target)
milestones.description: Updated grid-connection target after FID; first reactor expected to enter commercial operation late 2030s.

milestones.title: Second reactor target
milestones.description: Second EPR reactor; project rated at 3.2 GW combined.

TR010032.name: Lower Thames Crossing
TR010032.summary: A new road tunnel between Tilbury and Gravesend intended to relieve the Dartford Crossing, first mooted in the 1989 Roads for Prosperity white paper. After the 2002 ORBIT study and a 2009 DfT corridor analysis, Option C was selected in 2017; an initial DCO was withdrawn in 2020 before PINS could refuse it, the resubmitted DCO was examined in 2023 and granted on 25 March 2025 at an estimated cost of around £10 billion, with early works starting in 2026.

milestones.title: Roads for Prosperity floats new Thames crossing
milestones.description: White paper considers an additional crossing downstream of Dartford to relieve the M25.

milestones.title: ORBIT Multi-Modal Study
milestones.description: Orbital London study recommends a Lower Thames Crossing for further work.

milestones.title: DfT capacity study identifies five corridors
milestones.description: Five corridors examined; Options A, B and C taken forward for detailed evaluation.

milestones.title: Options consultation by DfT
milestones.description: Public consultation on options for a new Lower Thames Crossing.

milestones.title: Highways England route consultation
milestones.description: Two-month consultation drew over 47,000 responses, the largest UK road consultation.

milestones.title: Secretary of State confirms Option C
milestones.description: Chris Grayling selects Option C: a bored tunnel east of Gravesend and Tilbury.

milestones.title: First DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Highways England submits its initial Development Consent Order to PINS.

milestones.title: First DCO withdrawn
milestones.description: After PINS signals it will not accept the application, Highways England withdraws to do further work.

milestones.title: Resubmitted DCO accepted
milestones.description: National Highways submits revised DCO; pre-application costs by this point around £267m.

milestones.title: PINS examination opens
milestones.description: Six-month examination of the resubmitted application begins.

milestones.title: Decision deadline extended to May 2025
milestones.description: Following the general election, the Secretary of State extends the decision deadline.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: Secretary of State grants the A122 Lower Thames Crossing DCO; estimated cost ~£10bn.

milestones.title: Spending settlement confirms £3.1bn public funding
milestones.description: Government commits public funding alongside ~£7.5bn private finance, total ~£10.6bn; opening pushed to 2032-2034.

milestones.title: Early construction begins
milestones.description: Enabling works start at Coalhouse Fort, East Tilbury.

TR020003.name: Expansion of Heathrow Airport (Third Runway)
TR020003.summary: A 50-year saga of UK aviation capacity decisions. After the 1968-71 Roskill Commission rejected expansion at Heathrow in favour of a new airport, successive governments returned to Heathrow expansion: the 2003 White Paper supported it, Labour approved it in 2009, the Coalition cancelled it in 2010, the Davies Commission re-recommended it in 2015, Parliament designated the ANPS in 2018, the Court of Appeal quashed it in 2020 before the Supreme Court reinstated it later that year. In January 2025 Chancellor Rachel Reeves backed the scheme; in November 2025 the government adopted Heathrow's £49bn northwest runway proposal as the basis for a revised ANPS, with a DCO targeted for December 2027.

milestones.title: Roskill Commission established
milestones.description: Government commission set up to identify a site for London's third airport; Heathrow expansion not on shortlist.

milestones.title: Roskill report recommends Cublington
milestones.description: Commission recommends Cublington (Bucks); government rejects this in April 1971 and chooses Foulness/Maplin instead, later cancelled in 1974.

milestones.title: Fourth terminal approved at Heathrow
milestones.description: Following public inquiry, government approves Terminal 4, foreshadowing further expansion debate.

milestones.title: Aviation White Paper backs new Heathrow runway
milestones.description: 'The Future of Air Transport' (Darling) supports a third runway and sixth terminal at Heathrow.

milestones.title: Labour approves third runway
milestones.description: Transport Secretary Geoff Hoon announces support for a 2,200m third runway and new terminal.

milestones.title: Coalition cancels third runway
milestones.description: Conservative-Lib Dem coalition agreement scraps the scheme; David Cameron's 'no ifs, no buts' pledge.

milestones.title: Davies (Airports) Commission established
milestones.description: Government appoints Sir Howard Davies to chair an independent commission on UK airport capacity.

milestones.title: Davies interim report shortlists three options
milestones.description: Northwest Heathrow runway, extended-northern Heathrow runway and Gatwick second runway shortlisted.

milestones.title: Davies Commission recommends Heathrow NW runway
milestones.description: Commission unanimously recommends a new 3,500m northwest runway at Heathrow with a sixth terminal.

milestones.title: May government adopts NW runway
milestones.description: Theresa May's cabinet endorses Heathrow northwest runway; Zac Goldsmith resigns as MP in protest.

milestones.title: Airports National Policy Statement designated
milestones.description: House of Commons approves ANPS by 415 to 119; designated by Secretary of State 26 June 2018.

milestones.title: Friends of the Earth and Plan B file JR
milestones.description: Environmental groups challenge the ANPS on climate grounds.

milestones.title: Court of Appeal quashes ANPS on climate grounds
milestones.description: Court rules the ANPS unlawful for failing to take account of the Paris Agreement.

milestones.title: Supreme Court reinstates ANPS
milestones.description: Unanimously overturns Court of Appeal; ANPS lawful, Heathrow free to seek a DCO.

milestones.title: COVID and ownership changes pause scheme
milestones.description: Pandemic collapses traffic; Ferrovial exits in late 2023, selling 25% to Ardian and Saudi PIF.

milestones.title: Heathrow signals third-runway revival
milestones.description: Returning to profit, Heathrow says it wants to revive expansion plans under new ownership and CEO.

milestones.title: Chancellor Reeves backs third runway
milestones.description: Rachel Reeves's growth speech endorses a third runway as 'badly needed', invites a compliant proposal by summer 2025.

milestones.title: PINS publishes Scoping Opinion
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate issues Scoping Opinion; applicant moves to enhanced pre-application service.

milestones.title: Government adopts HAL £49bn NW runway scheme
milestones.description: Heidi Alexander confirms HAL's privately financed scheme as the basis for a revised ANPS; DCO targeted for December 2027, operational by 2035.

EN010120.name: Drax Bioenergy with Carbon Capture and Storage Project
EN010120.summary: Drax Power Limited's proposal to retrofit post-combustion carbon capture to two of four biomass units at the former coal-fired Drax Power Station, targeting ~8 Mt/yr of negative emissions by 2030. The £2bn project received development consent in January 2024 but faces a judicial review, ongoing controversies over biomass sourcing, and unresolved subsidy arrangements beyond 2031.

milestones.title: First coal unit synchronised
milestones.description: Drax Power Station's first 660 MW coal unit commissioned by the CEGB; full 4 GW six-unit station completed by 1986.

milestones.title: Biomass conversion programme announced
milestones.description: Drax announces conversion of three units from coal to imported wood pellets under the Renewables Obligation.

milestones.title: BECCS pilot partnership announced
milestones.description: Drax partners with Leeds spin-out C-Capture to trial biomass carbon capture solvent technology.

milestones.title: BECCS pilot captures first CO2
milestones.description: World-first capture of CO2 from 100% biomass combustion at the pilot plant; Drax pledges carbon-negative by 2030.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted to PINS
milestones.description: Drax submits application EN010120 to retrofit carbon capture on two biomass units.

milestones.title: BBC Panorama investigation airs
milestones.description: Panorama alleges Drax sources wood pellets from primary and old-growth forests in British Columbia.

milestones.title: Drax pauses £2bn BECCS investment
milestones.description: Project excluded from CCS Track 1 cluster funding; Drax suspends further capital spend pending government clarity on support.

milestones.title: Ofgem opens RO sustainability investigation
milestones.description: Ofgem investigates Drax's annual profiling submissions for the Renewables Obligation scheme.

milestones.title: DCO granted
milestones.description: Energy Secretary Claire Coutinho approves consent to retrofit BECCS on Units 1 and 2; capital cost ~£2bn, target 8 Mt CO2/yr captured.

milestones.title: Biofuelwatch lodges High Court challenge
milestones.description: Judicial review alleges the DCO breached EIA regulations by treating biomass emissions as zero and scoping out CO2 transport and storage.

milestones.title: Ofgem fines Drax £25m for misreporting
milestones.description: Ofgem finds inaccurate sustainability reporting on Canadian biomass; £25m paid into Voluntary Redress Fund and supply-chain audit ordered.

milestones.title: Bridging mechanism Heads of Terms agreed
milestones.description: Government and Drax agree a four-year low-carbon dispatchable CfD (April 2027 to March 2031) at £113/MWh, halving subsidies.

TR010018.name: A14 Cambridge to Huntingdon Improvement Scheme
TR010018.summary: A 21-mile strategic upgrade between Cambridge and Huntingdon, including a new Huntingdon southern bypass and widening of the A1 between Brampton and Alconbury. The project evolved from the earlier A14 Ellington to Fen Ditton scheme (preferred route 2007), was cancelled in 2010 by the Coalition, revived as a partly-tolled scheme in 2013, granted development consent in 2016, and opened to traffic in May 2020.

milestones.title: Initial public consultation (Ellington to Fen Ditton)
milestones.description: Highways Agency held first public consultation in spring 2005 on proposals for upgrading the A14 between Ellington and Fen Ditton, the predecessor scheme.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced for Ellington to Fen Drayton section
milestones.description: Highways Agency confirmed the preferred route and strategy for the western section of the A14 upgrade.

milestones.title: Coalition cancels scheme in Spending Review
milestones.description: Transport Secretary Philip Hammond and Roads Minister Mike Penning declared the scheme unaffordable in the 2010 Spending Review.

milestones.title: A14 Challenge launched
milestones.description: DfT, with Cambridgeshire and other councils, launched the A14 Challenge inviting proposals to address congestion on the corridor.

milestones.title: Scheme relaunched as £1.5bn improvement (with proposed toll)
milestones.description: Highways Agency unveiled a revived £1.5bn scheme including a new 12-mile Huntingdon southern bypass.

milestones.title: Toll plans dropped
milestones.description: Government announced road charging on the A14 would not proceed; the scheme would be Exchequer-funded.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Highways Agency lodged its DCO application for TR010018 with the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Examination begins
milestones.description: A four-member Examining Panel opened the six-month statutory examination.

milestones.title: Development consent granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport made the A14 Cambridge to Huntingdon DCO 2016.

milestones.title: Main construction starts
milestones.description: Highways England began main construction works on the 21-mile upgrade, with peak workforce of around 2,500.

milestones.title: Upgraded A14 fully opened to traffic
milestones.description: The new dual three-lane A14 including the Huntingdon southern bypass opened eight months ahead of the December 2020 schedule.

milestones.title: Final scheme works completed
milestones.description: Mill Common link road opened in June 2022, ending five and a half years of construction.

TR010021.name: Silvertown Tunnel
TR010021.summary: TfL's 1.4 km twin-bore Thames road tunnel between Silvertown and the Greenwich Peninsula, conceived after the 2008 cancellation of the Thames Gateway Bridge. Granted development consent in May 2018 and built by the Riverlinx consortium under a design-build-finance-maintain contract, it opened on 7 April 2025 with user charges that also for the first time tolled the adjacent Blackwall Tunnel.

milestones.title: Thames Gateway Bridge cancelled
milestones.description: Mayor Boris Johnson cancelled the proposed Thames Gateway Bridge, prompting TfL to refocus east London river crossing strategy on a tunnel option at Silvertown.

milestones.title: First public consultation
milestones.description: TfL launched its first public consultation on the Silvertown Tunnel proposal alongside the Gallions Reach Ferry option.

milestones.title: Toll consultation launched
milestones.description: TfL consulted on user charges for both the proposed Silvertown Tunnel and the existing Blackwall Tunnel.

milestones.title: DCO application accepted
milestones.description: TfL's Silvertown Tunnel Order application was accepted for examination by PINS on 31 May 2016.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: PINS began its six-month examination of the Silvertown Tunnel DCO on 11 October 2016.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: The Secretary of State for Transport made The Silvertown Tunnel Order 2018, granting development consent.

milestones.title: Judicial review threatened by opponents
milestones.description: The No to Silvertown Tunnel coalition formally threatened JR on environmental and air-quality grounds following award of the £1bn DBFM contract.

milestones.title: Riverlinx contract awarded
milestones.description: TfL closed the design-build-finance-maintain contract with the Riverlinx consortium (Cintra, BAM, Macquarie Capital, SK E&C).

milestones.title: Construction commences
milestones.description: Main construction works began on the tunnel and approach roads at Silvertown and the Greenwich Peninsula.

milestones.title: Tunnel boring begins
milestones.description: Herrenknecht TBM 'Jill' began boring; the southbound drive completed Feb 2023 and northbound drive completed Sep 2023.

milestones.title: Tunnel opens with user charges
milestones.description: The Silvertown Tunnel opened to traffic at 06:00; tolling began simultaneously on both Silvertown and the previously free Blackwall Tunnel, drawing a 45,000-signature petition.

TR010036.name: A303 Sparkford to Ilchester Dualling
TR010036.summary: A 3-mile dualling of the last single-carriageway section of the A303 between the Podimore and Sparkford bypasses in Somerset. Originally subject to Highways Act orders in 1995 alongside 1990s A303 corridor schemes that were cancelled in 1998, the current scheme was redeveloped from 2015, granted development consent in January 2021 after a first-of-its-kind 'minded to refuse' letter and a Transport Action Network judicial review of RIS2, and opened to traffic in November 2024.

milestones.title: Original Highways Act orders made
milestones.description: Orders under the Highways Act 1980 made in 1995 as part of wider A303 corridor dualling ambitions. Adjacent schemes cancelled in 1998.

milestones.title: Scheme redeveloped under RIS1
milestones.description: Highways England began developing a new dualling proposal under the first Road Investment Strategy.

milestones.title: Options public consultation
milestones.description: Highways England consulted on two route options; 64% preferred Option 1.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: Highways England announced Option 1 as the preferred route for the £179m dualling.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Highways England submitted the DCO; accepted for examination 23 August 2018.

milestones.title: Examination commences
milestones.description: Six-month DCO examination ran from December 2018 to June 2019; ExA report 12 September 2019.

milestones.title: 'Minded to refuse' + TAN judicial review
milestones.description: DfT issued a first-of-its-kind 'minded to refuse' letter on RNAS Yeovilton bird-strike risks; in parallel TAN's RIS2 challenge was rejected by Holgate J.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted the DCO after National Highways addressed the 'minded to refuse' issues.

milestones.title: Main construction begins
milestones.description: Galliford Try began main construction works under a contract worth ~£135m; scheme cost ~£250m.

milestones.title: Dualled road opens to traffic
milestones.description: The new three-mile dual carriageway opened to traffic on 4 November 2024, delayed from the original Spring 2024 target.

TR010044.name: A428 Black Cat to Caxton Gibbet Road Improvement scheme
TR010044.summary: A long-anticipated upgrade of the central Cambridge-Bedford A428 to dual carriageway, completing a strategic link the eastern (Caxton Common to Hardwick) section of which was dualled in 2007. After being added to RIS1 in 2014, the scheme progressed through consultation (2017), preferred route announcement (2019), DCO consent (August 2022) and a 2022-23 judicial review, with main construction beginning in December 2023 and opening expected in spring 2027.

milestones.title: Eastern section dualled
milestones.description: The £55m A428 Caxton Common to Hardwick dualling opened on 24 May 2007, leaving the Black Cat to Caxton Gibbet section as single carriageway.

milestones.title: Included in Road Investment Strategy 1
milestones.description: DfT's first Road Investment Strategy committed to planning the A1-to-A1198 dualling, including replacing the Black Cat roundabout with a grade-separated junction.

milestones.title: Public consultation on three route options
milestones.description: Highways England consulted on three corridor options (Orange, Purple and Pink) plus three Black Cat junction options.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: Highways England confirmed the Orange route as the preferred option on 18 February 2019.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: National Highways submitted the DCO on 26 February 2021; accepted for examination 23 March. Skanska awarded the construction contract.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted development consent on 18 August 2022 (SI 2022 No. 934).

milestones.title: Judicial review conclusively refused
milestones.description: Transport Action Network's challenge on biodiversity, need and climate grounds was finally refused by the Court of Appeal on 18 May 2023.

milestones.title: Main construction starts
milestones.description: Skanska's main construction works began on 12 December 2023, backed by around £1bn of government investment.

milestones.title: Scheme reaches halfway point
milestones.description: By October 2025 Skanska reported the £1bn project was 50% complete.

milestones.title: Expected opening to traffic
milestones.description: National Highways expects the new dual carriageway to open to traffic in spring 2027.

TR010065.name: A46 Newark Bypass
TR010065.summary: The A46 Newark Bypass is a £686m National Highways scheme to dual the remaining 6.5km single-carriageway gap between Farndon and Winthorpe. The original Newark relief road opened in 1990; dualling was committed to in the 2014 Autumn Statement and RIS1 (2015), with development consent granted on 1 October 2025 after examination. Construction is expected to begin in 2026 and complete within RIS3 (2026-2031).

milestones.title: Scheme included in Autumn Statement and RIS1
milestones.description: Earlier A46 dualling left the existing Newark relief road (1990, single carriageway) as the 'single greatest gap' in the Midlands Trade Corridor; the 2014 Autumn Statement and RIS1 (March 2015) committed funding to dual the bypass.

milestones.title: Options public consultation
milestones.description: National Highways ran an eight-week consultation on route options (Dec 2020 - Feb 2021), drawing more than 1,500 responses.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: National Highways announced 'Option 2 Modified' as the preferred route in February 2022.

milestones.title: Skanska awarded design-and-build contract
milestones.description: Skanska signed a £345m design-and-build contract with National Highways, subject to development consent.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted and accepted
milestones.description: National Highways submitted the DCO on 26 April 2024; accepted for examination on 23 May 2024.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: The Preliminary Meeting and first Open Floor Hearing took place on 8 October 2024; examination concluded April 2025.

milestones.title: Secretary of State grants DCO
milestones.description: Following the ExA's recommendation on 1 July 2025, the SoS for Transport granted development consent for the £686m scheme on 1 October 2025.

milestones.title: Construction expected to start
milestones.description: Skanska is progressing detailed design; construction expected to start in 2026, completion targeted within RIS3 (2026-2031).

TR010054.name: M54 to M6 Link Road
TR010054.summary: A 2.5 km dual-carriageway link between M54 Junction 1 and M6 Junction 11 in South Staffordshire, intended to provide the missing direct northbound motorway connection omitted when the M6 Toll opened in 2003. First mooted in the mid-1980s as part of the Western Orbital corridor and supported in principle in 2003, the scheme was revived in the 2014 RIS, granted DCO in April 2022, and is now funded under RIS3 (2026-2031) with construction expected to begin by 2027.

milestones.title: Original M54-BNRR link proposed
milestones.description: A direct link in this corridor was first proposed in the mid-1980s as part of the A446(M)/Western Orbital scheme connecting the M54 to the planned Birmingham Northern Relief Road.

milestones.title: Link dropped from M6 Toll scheme
milestones.description: During the 1994-95 BNRR public inquiry the M54 link was removed; M6 Toll opened in 2003 with no direct M54 connection.

milestones.title: Secretary of State supports link in principle
milestones.description: Privately-funded link from M54 to the M6 Toll endorsed, to be funded by Macquarie Infrastructure Group.

milestones.title: Macquarie-funded scheme displayed
milestones.description: Plans for a £102m Macquarie-funded link to M6 Toll Junction 11a put on public display; the privately-funded scheme did not proceed.

milestones.title: Included in Road Investment Strategy 1
milestones.description: Committed to in the December 2014 RIS as a publicly-funded scheme.

milestones.title: Route options consultation
milestones.description: Highways England consulted on three route options for a new dual carriageway between M54 J1 and M6 J11.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced (Option B West)
milestones.description: Around three-quarters of consultation respondents supported Option B West.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Highways England submitted the DCO; accepted for examination on 28 February 2020.

milestones.title: DCO granted at second attempt
milestones.description: Decision extended from October 2021 due to environmental concerns; SoS granted the £200m scheme on 21 April 2022.

milestones.title: Funding confirmed in Spending Review
milestones.description: Following uncertainty post-DCO, the June 2025 Spending Review reconfirmed funding for the £200m scheme.

milestones.title: Included in RIS3 (2026-2031)
milestones.description: DfT published RIS3 listing the M54 to M6 Link Road for delivery during 2026-2031.

milestones.title: Construction expected to begin
milestones.description: National Highways expects main construction to begin by late 2026/early 2027; DCO requires works to commence by 2027.

milestones.title: Expected completion
milestones.description: Targeted for the early 2030s; expected to remove around 19,000 vehicles per day from the A460 and other local roads.

TR010056.name: A417 Missing Link
TR010056.summary: A 30-year-old proposal to dual the ~3-mile single-carriageway A417 pinch-point between Brockworth and Cowley (Crickley Hill, the Air Balloon roundabout, Birdlip). Earlier 1990s schemes were deferred under successive roads policy reviews; revived under RIS1, consulted on three times (2018-2020), granted development consent on 16 November 2022, and now under construction by Kier with completion targeted for 2027.

milestones.title: Early Brockworth-area dualling proposals
milestones.description: DoT published a draft route in January 1991; the Crickley Hill / Birdlip stretch was not progressed at the time due to AONB and service-area objections.

milestones.title: Brockworth Bypass opens
milestones.description: The 3-mile Brockworth Bypass and new M5 J11a opened in December 1995, leaving Crickley Hill, Air Balloon and Birdlip as a bottleneck.

milestones.title: Dropped under New Labour Roads Review
milestones.description: Prescott's 'A New Deal for Trunk Roads in England' cut the national roads programme; the gap remained known as the 'missing link'.

milestones.title: Included in Road Investment Strategy 1
milestones.description: Identified for development in RIS1 (2015-2020) as part of the strategic upgrade of the A417/A419 corridor.

milestones.title: First non-statutory public consultation
milestones.description: Highways England held its first public consultation on options for closing the missing link.

milestones.title: Preferred Route Announcement (Option 30)
milestones.description: A 3.6-mile dual carriageway running east of Crickley Hill through Shab Hill.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted and accepted
milestones.description: National Highways submitted the DCO on 1 June 2021; PINS accepted for examination on 29 June 2021.

milestones.title: Kier awarded £460m construction contract
milestones.description: Kier confirmed as main works contractor in April 2022.

milestones.title: Development consent granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted the DCO; SI 2022 No. 1248. The six-week JR window ran without a successful challenge.

milestones.title: Main construction begins
milestones.description: Site clearance and preliminary works began in 2023; the Air Balloon pub closed on 31 December 2022 and was demolished in December 2023.

milestones.title: Scheme open to traffic (target)
milestones.description: National Highways' programme targets opening of the new dual carriageway in 2027.

TR010062.name: A66 Northern Trans-Pennine Project
TR010062.summary: Long-running programme to dual the remaining single-carriageway sections of the A66 between M6 J40 (Penrith) and the A1(M) at Scotch Corner. After piecemeal bypass dualling from the 1970s and a post-2010 funding pause, the scheme was revived through the 2015 Trans-Pennine study, accelerated as a Project Speed pathfinder in 2020, granted DCO in March 2024, and survived a Transport Action Network judicial review by January 2025; construction is scheduled to begin in 2026.

milestones.title: Coalition Spending Review pauses outstanding A66 dualling
milestones.description: Earlier piecemeal A66 dualling progressed via village-bypass schemes from 1971 (Penrith), 1977 (Brough), 1980 (Greta Bridge), 1992 (Stainmore), 1993 (Bowes Moor) and 2007-08 (Temple Sowerby). Following the October 2010 Spending Review, the remaining single-carriageway sections were shelved.

milestones.title: Trans-Pennine connectivity study funded
milestones.description: Government allocated £500,000 to study the A66 and A69 trans-Pennine routes.

milestones.title: A66 selected for full dualling
milestones.description: Full dualling between M6 J40 and A1(M) Scotch Corner entered the next Road Investment Strategy pipeline.

milestones.title: Options consultation
milestones.description: National Highways ran a public options consultation in mid-2019; 92% supported completing the dualling.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: DfT announced the preferred route in May 2020, setting eight individual scheme alignments along the 80 km corridor.

milestones.title: Designated a Project Speed pathfinder
milestones.description: Following Boris Johnson's 'build, build, build' speech, the A66 was named a Project Speed pathfinder, halving construction time but later linked to a ~28% cost increase.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: National Highways submitted the DCO on 21 June 2022; accepted for examination 19 July 2022.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: Mark Harper made the A66 Northern Trans-Pennine DCO 2024 (SI 2024/360) on 7 March 2024, authorising the £1.3bn scheme.

milestones.title: TAN files judicial review
milestones.description: Transport Action Network lodged a statutory challenge before the 18 April 2024 deadline arguing failures on Habitats Regulations Assessment, AONB, BCR and carbon.

milestones.title: High Court refuses permission
milestones.description: The High Court dismissed TAN's challenge, finding none of its grounds arguable.

milestones.title: Court of Appeal dismisses appeal
milestones.description: Court of Appeal refused TAN permission to appeal, exhausting the JR process.

milestones.title: Confirmed in Spending Review and RIS3
milestones.description: After a Labour-government review, confirmed within the June 2025 Spending Review and the £27bn RIS3.

milestones.title: Main construction expected to start
milestones.description: DfT and National Highways have confirmed main dualling works scheduled to begin in 2026.

milestones.title: Target full opening
milestones.description: Project Speed accelerated programme targets full opening of the dualled 80 km route in 2029.

TR010055.name: M3 Junction 9 Improvement
TR010055.summary: National Highways scheme to remodel the M3/A34 interchange at Winnall, Winchester, with free-flow slip roads, underpasses, and widening of the M3 to four lanes. After three rounds of consultation (2018, 2019, 2021), the DCO was granted on 16 May 2024 and main construction is underway under a £290m budget for completion by late 2027.

milestones.title: First non-statutory consultation
milestones.description: Highways England unveiled the M3 Junction 9 plans on 9 January 2018, with public consultation running until 19 February.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation
milestones.description: An eight-week statutory consultation under the Planning Act 2008 ran from 2 July to 27 August 2019.

milestones.title: Supplementary preliminary design consultation
milestones.description: Six-week supplementary consultation (May-July 2021) on revisions to the preliminary design.

milestones.title: VolkerFitzpatrick selected as principal contractor
milestones.description: National Highways announced VolkerFitzpatrick as principal contractor in December 2022, ahead of DCO submission.

milestones.title: DCO accepted and examination begins
milestones.description: PINS accepted the application; examination of relevant representations ran from 1 February to 13 March 2023.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted the DCO on 16 May 2024 (S.I. 2024 No. 752).

milestones.title: Early enabling works begin
milestones.description: VolkerFitzpatrick started enabling works on 11 October 2024.

milestones.title: £290m funding confirmed by ministers
milestones.description: Scheme reaffirmed on 13 March 2025 with £290m of government funding, supporting over 2,000 planned homes in Winchester.

milestones.title: New bridge beams installed
milestones.description: Construction milestone reached in February 2026 under the VolkerFitzpatrick / Balfour Beatty JV.

milestones.title: Scheme completion expected
milestones.description: All works expected to be completed by late 2027.

TR010031.name: A1 Birtley to Coal House Improvement Scheme
TR010031.summary: A 6.5km widening of the A1 south of Gateshead between junctions 65 (Birtley) and 67 (Coal House), including offline replacement of Allerdene Railway Bridge over the East Coast Main Line. Granted Development Consent in January 2021 as the Planning Inspectorate's 100th NSIP decision; construction commenced in 2021 and most lanes opened by December 2024, but defective welds identified by steel manufacturer Severfield in August 2024 have delayed final completion.

milestones.title: Initial options consultation
milestones.description: Highways England held an options consultation on widening proposals between August and October 2016.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation on preferred scheme
milestones.description: Detailed statutory consultation between February and March 2018 ahead of DCO submission.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Highways England submitted its DCO on 14 August 2019; accepted for examination 10 September 2019.

milestones.title: Examination concludes
milestones.description: ExA recommendation report submitted on 21 October 2020. Antony Gormley contributed views on protecting the adjacent Angel of the North.

milestones.title: Development Consent granted (PINS' 100th NSIP)
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted the DCO on 19 January 2021 — PINS' 100th NSIP decision, with additional requirements around the Angel of the North.

milestones.title: Construction commenced
milestones.description: Main works started July-September 2021; project value ~£250m.

milestones.title: New Allerdene Bridge opened to traffic
milestones.description: Replacement Allerdene Railway Bridge over the ECML opened in September 2023.

milestones.title: Severfield weld defects disclosed
milestones.description: Steel manufacturer Severfield disclosed welding quality defects affecting Allerdene Bridge among 13 structures; lane restrictions remained in force.

milestones.title: Majority of new lanes opened
milestones.description: Most widened carriageway open by December 2024; lane restrictions remained on the Allerdene Bridge section pending weld remediation.

milestones.title: Full scheme completion (expected)
milestones.description: Final completion contingent on Allerdene Bridge weld remediation.

TR010029.name: M25 junction 28 improvements
TR010029.summary: Upgrade of the M25/A12 Brook Street Interchange near Brentwood, adding a dedicated two-lane free-flow loop road from the M25 anti-clockwise to the A12 eastbound. The original Brook Street Roundabout opened 1965, was widened in 2008. National Highways obtained development consent in May 2022 after consultations from 2016-2019, awarded a £124M contract to GRAHAM, and opened the new loop road in summer 2025 as part of the £170M scheme.

milestones.title: First non-statutory consultation
milestones.description: Highways England ran initial consultation Nov 2016 - Jan 2017 on options to upgrade the existing Brook Street roundabout (originally opened 1965). 228 responses received.

milestones.title: Preferred route announcement (Option 5F)
milestones.description: Two-lane loop road from M25 anti-clockwise to A12 eastbound announced as preferred.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation
milestones.description: Eight-week statutory consultation Dec 2018 - Jan 2019.

milestones.title: DCO accepted for examination
milestones.description: Application submitted 27 May 2020; PINS accepted on 24 June 2020 as the 112th NSIP.

milestones.title: ExA recommendation submitted
milestones.description: ExA recommendation submitted to SoS on 16 September 2021.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted the DCO on 16 May 2022.

milestones.title: GRAHAM begins construction
milestones.description: GRAHAM began enabling and main construction works under a £124M design-and-build contract.

milestones.title: Loop road and full scheme open
milestones.description: New free-flow loop road from M25 anti-clockwise to A12 eastbound opened; £170M scheme reached full completion late summer 2025.

TR010022.name: A38 Derby Junctions
TR010022.summary: Grade-separated upgrades to three Derby A38 roundabouts (Kingsway, Markeaton, Little Eaton), originating in the 2014 Road Investment Strategy. The scheme's January 2021 DCO was quashed in the High Court two months later for an unlawful climate assessment, redetermined and re-granted in August 2023, then survived a second JR in August 2024. With legal challenges resolved, National Highways launched market engagement in February 2026 for a £600M, 10-year delivery contract running 2028-2038.

milestones.title: Included in Road Investment Strategy 1
milestones.description: Committed to in the December 2014 RIS1 alongside short-term improvements at Little Eaton and Markeaton.

milestones.title: Non-statutory consultation
milestones.description: Highways England ran a non-statutory consultation Jan-March 2015.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: Underpasses at Kingsway and Markeaton; flyover at Little Eaton.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation
milestones.description: Statutory consultation September-October 2018.

milestones.title: DCO accepted for examination
milestones.description: PINS accepted the DCO on 21 May 2019.

milestones.title: Examination begins
milestones.description: Examination opened 8 October 2019, concluded 8 January 2020.

milestones.title: Original DCO granted
milestones.description: Grant Shapps granted the DCO on 8 January 2021, authorising the £250M scheme.

milestones.title: DCO quashed by High Court
milestones.description: Mia Bains and Stop the A38 Expansion JR succeeded; SoS conceded the climate assessment was unlawful.

milestones.title: DCO re-granted after redetermination
milestones.description: Mark Harper re-granted the DCO on 17 August 2023 after addressing the carbon assessment deficiencies.

milestones.title: Second judicial review dismissed
milestones.description: Mr Justice Mould dismissed the second JR which argued the scheme was approved without an up-to-date economic assessment.

milestones.title: Site surveys commence
milestones.description: With legal challenges resolved, National Highways began wildlife and environmental site surveys.

milestones.title: Market engagement for £600M delivery contract
milestones.description: National Highways issued a preliminary market engagement notice on 20 February 2026 for a £600M, 10-year design-and-build contract.

milestones.title: Construction contract starts
milestones.description: The 10-year design-and-build contract is scheduled to run from 31 January 2028 to 30 January 2038.

TR010030.name: M25 junction 10/A3 Wisley interchange improvement
TR010030.summary: Three-level Wisley Interchange opened in 1983 and became one of the busiest sections of the M25 with around 300,000 vehicles a day. National Highways consulted on options from 2016, secured DCO consent in May 2022, and began construction later that year; the rebuilt junction reopened to traffic in January 2026 with the wider scheme due to complete in spring 2026.

milestones.title: Identified for upgrade in RIS1
milestones.description: RIS1 lists M25 Junction 10/A3 Wisley as a priority scheme citing chronic congestion and a poor safety record.

milestones.title: First public consultation on options
milestones.description: Highways England consulted Dec 2016 - Feb 2017 on flyover (Option 9) vs enlarged roundabout (Option 14).

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: Option 14 — an elongated roundabout with new slip roads and widened A3 — confirmed as the preferred scheme.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Highways England submitted the DCO; accepted for examination 17 July 2019.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Six-month examination begins; RHS Wisley, Wisley Action Group and Ockham Parish Council submit a joint statement of case opposing the scheme. Extended due to COVID, closing in July 2020.

milestones.title: Decision deadline extended
milestones.description: SoS extended the decision deadline several times through 2021, citing impacts on Thames Basin Heaths SPA and RHS Garden Wisley.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: Grant Shapps granted the DCO, including the UK's first heathland green bridge.

milestones.title: Construction begins
milestones.description: Balfour Beatty started main works on the £300m+ scheme; budget later rose to around £370m.

milestones.title: First full M25 closure since 1986
milestones.description: National Highways closed a section of the M25 for the first time since the motorway opened to install bridge structures at Junction 10.

milestones.title: Rebuilt junction fully reopens
milestones.description: All movements at the rebuilt Wisley Interchange open to traffic, three years after construction started.

milestones.title: Wider scheme completion
milestones.description: Landscaping, heathland restoration and the heathland green bridge expected to complete in spring 2026.

TR010008.name: Heysham to M6 Link Road
TR010008.summary: The Heysham to M6 Link Road (Bay Gateway) is a 4.8 km dual carriageway connecting M6 Junction 34 to the Morecambe and Heysham peninsula. First identified in the 1948 Road Plan for Lancashire, it spent over six decades in planning before becoming one of the first highway schemes to receive development consent under the Planning Act 2008 NSIP regime in March 2013. Construction was led by Costain and the road opened on 31 October 2016 at a final cost of £123.9 million.

milestones.title: Link identified in Road Plan for Lancashire
milestones.description: The need for a new road connecting Heysham and Morecambe to the future M6 corridor was first formally proposed in the 1948 Road Plan.

milestones.title: Reaffirmed in Lancashire Development Plan
milestones.description: Reaffirmed in the 1962 Lancashire Development Plan; no construction followed.

milestones.title: First phase opens (Heysham bypass)
milestones.description: The Heysham bypass between Heysham Harbour and the A589 Morecambe Road opened in July 1994, leaving the River Lune crossing unbuilt.

milestones.title: Western bypass route rejected
milestones.description: Independent inspector recommended deleting the western bypass option on landscape and estuary grounds; LCC began examining alternatives.

milestones.title: Northern route selected
milestones.description: LCC selected the shorter northern route crossing the Lune to a remodelled M6 J34.

milestones.title: DfT confirms £111m funding; DCO route authorised
milestones.description: DfT confirmed funding; LCC authorised preparation of an application to the IPC under the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: First highway and local-authority scheme to receive consent under the Planning Act 2008 NSIP regime.

milestones.title: High Court refuses judicial review
milestones.description: Transport Solutions for Lancaster and Morecambe (TSLM) challenge dismissed on all five grounds.

milestones.title: Court of Appeal dismisses final challenge
milestones.description: Final appeal dismissed; legal hurdles cleared.

milestones.title: Construction begins (Costain)
milestones.description: Costain began main works after Oxford Archaeology North archaeological investigations.

milestones.title: Bay Gateway opens to traffic
milestones.description: Opened at a final cost of £123.9m; over 18,000 vehicles per day in the first year.

TR010027.name: M42 Junction 6 Improvement
TR010027.summary: National Highways scheme to upgrade M42 Junction 6 serving Birmingham Airport, the NEC and the future HS2 Interchange station at Arden Cross. The DCO was granted on 21 May 2020 and Skanska delivered the c.£143m construction contract, with the new junction 5a and A4545 link opening on 18 December 2024.

milestones.title: Initial public consultation
milestones.description: Highways England began consultation on options to improve M42 Junction 6 to support access to Birmingham Airport, the NEC and the planned HS2 Interchange.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: Highways England announced the preferred route — a new dual carriageway link between the Clock Interchange and a new junction on the M42, plus free-flow links.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation
milestones.description: Statutory consultation 9 January to 9 March 2018.

milestones.title: DCO application accepted
milestones.description: Highways England submitted the DCO on 2 January 2019; PINS accepted on 30 January 2019.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted development consent on 21 May 2020.

milestones.title: Skanska given Notice to Proceed
milestones.description: Skanska awarded the c.£143m main works contract; main construction began shortly after.

milestones.title: Major bridges installed over M42
milestones.description: Two bridges weighing a combined 2,050 tonnes installed over the M42 during a weekend closure.

milestones.title: Junction 5a and A4545 link road open
milestones.description: New 2.4km A4545 dual carriageway link and M42 J5a opened to traffic.

TR010034.name: A57 Link Roads (previously known as Trans Pennine Upgrade Programme)
TR010034.summary: One of the longest-running road schemes in the UK. First proposed locally in 1971 and successively revived, suspended, cancelled and revived again across five decades, the scheme was finally consented as the A57 Link Roads DCO on 16 November 2022, survived a CPRE judicial review in 2023-2024, and entered main construction in early 2025 with completion expected around 2028-2029.

milestones.title: Local campaigning begins
milestones.description: Tameside residents and politicians begin lobbying for an A57/A628 bypass of Mottram, Hollingworth and Tintwistle.

milestones.title: Included in Roads for Prosperity
milestones.description: Conservative government's Roads for Prosperity white paper restores the bypass to the national roads programme.

milestones.title: Preferred route selected
milestones.description: Highways Agency announces preferred route for the Mottram-Hollingworth-Tintwistle bypass.

milestones.title: Scheme suspended
milestones.description: Bypass suspended as part of a wider review of the national road-building programme.

milestones.title: New Deal for Trunk Roads
milestones.description: Labour government's review returns the bypass to the preparatory pipeline.

milestones.title: Targeted Programme of Improvements
milestones.description: Following a 9,000-signature petition, the scheme enters DfT's Targeted Programme of Improvements.

milestones.title: Draft Orders published
milestones.description: 13-week consultation begins; environmental statement attracts 1,400 objections.

milestones.title: Public inquiry opens
milestones.description: Public inquiry opens; subsequently adjourned multiple times through 2007-2008.

milestones.title: Highways Agency withdraws from inquiry
milestones.description: After a four-year delay and repeated adjournments, Highways Agency withdraws — effectively cancelling the scheme.

milestones.title: Reapproved with £170m commitment
milestones.description: RIS1 commits to a redesigned Mottram Bypass and Glossop Spur.

milestones.title: Trans-Pennine Upgrade package announced
milestones.description: Highways England announces a Trans-Pennine Upgrade package including link roads at Mottram and a longer-term tunnel study.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: DCO submitted to PINS; accepted for examination on 26 July 2021.

milestones.title: Development consent granted
milestones.description: SoS grants the A57 Link Roads DCO authorising a new dual-carriageway Mottram Moor Link Road and single-carriageway A57 link to Woolley Bridge.

milestones.title: CPRE judicial review launched
milestones.description: CPRE Peak District and South Yorkshire files a judicial review challenging the carbon assessment.

milestones.title: High Court dismisses challenge
milestones.description: Mrs Justice Thornton dismisses Ground 2; Ground 1 was stayed pending the Court of Appeal's Boswell judgment.

milestones.title: Final legal challenge ends
milestones.description: CPRE trustees decide not to appeal to the Court of Appeal; case closed.

milestones.title: Funding confirmed in Autumn Budget
milestones.description: Chancellor Reeves confirms funding for the £250m A57 Link Roads scheme.

milestones.title: Main construction begins
milestones.description: Balfour Beatty starts main works on the Mottram Moor Link Road and A57 Link Road.

milestones.title: Expected completion
milestones.description: National Highways targets completion around 2028, though some reporting suggests slippage to 2029.

TR010063.name: M5 Junction 10 Improvements Scheme
TR010063.summary: Gloucestershire County Council's scheme to convert M5 Junction 10 (between Cheltenham and Tewkesbury) into an all-movements junction, build a new west Cheltenham link road, and widen the A4019 Tewkesbury Road. Funded by the Housing Infrastructure Fund to unlock planned housing growth. DCO granted by SoS on 4 June 2025; main construction starts autumn 2026 with Galliford Try, completion targeted for 2028.

milestones.title: Options consultation
milestones.description: Non-statutory options consultation in autumn 2020; 84% of respondents agreed there was a clear need for the scheme.

milestones.title: Preferred route announcement
milestones.description: Option 2 announced as the preferred route — a grade-separated roundabout centred on the existing junction.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation
milestones.description: Ten-week statutory consultation Dec 2021 - Feb 2022 covering the junction upgrade, west Cheltenham link road and A4019 widening.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: GCC submitted the DCO on 19 December 2023; accepted for examination 16 January 2024.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Six-month DCO examination began on 4 June 2024.

milestones.title: Development consent granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted the DCO; correction order issued on 29 October 2025.

milestones.title: Additional £71.5m funding secured
milestones.description: UK Government confirmed an additional £71.5m enabling main construction to commence later in the year.

milestones.title: Main construction begins
milestones.description: Main works phase scheduled to begin autumn 2026 with Galliford Try.

milestones.title: Scheme completion targeted
milestones.description: Junction, link road and A4019 widening expected to open in 2028.

TR010002.name: A556 Knutsford to Bowdon Scheme
TR010002.summary: A 7.5km dual carriageway linking M6 Junction 19 to M56 Junction 7 in Cheshire. The corridor was on the trunk-road programme from the late 1980s, originally proposed as the A556(M) motorway in 1996, then re-cast as an all-purpose dual carriageway after the motorway scheme was abandoned in 2003. It became the first Highways Agency project to receive a Development Consent Order under the Planning Act 2008, granted in August 2014, and opened to traffic on 6 March 2017 at a cost of £192 million.

milestones.title: Scheme enters trunk-road programme
milestones.description: Upgrade between Knutsford and Bowdon first listed on the Major Trunk Road programme around 1987.

milestones.title: A556(M) motorway scheme made
milestones.description: 1996 statutory instrument formalised plans to upgrade the corridor to motorway standard.

milestones.title: Motorway upgrade rejected
milestones.description: Secretary of State rejected the motorway upgrade, prompting an alternative all-purpose dual carriageway scheme.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: Highways Agency announced the preferred route for an all-purpose dual carriageway bypass.

milestones.title: Statutory public consultation
milestones.description: Statutory pre-application consultation Jan-April 2012.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: First Highways Agency road DCO application; accepted for examination 17 May 2013.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: First DCO for a Highways Agency road project; no legal challenge.

milestones.title: Construction begins
milestones.description: Costain began construction of the new 7.5km dual carriageway.

milestones.title: New A556 opens to traffic
milestones.description: £192m dual carriageway between M6 J19 and M56 J7 opened to traffic.

TR010015.name: Norwich Northern Distributor Road (NDR)
TR010015.summary: Norfolk County Council's 20 km dual carriageway around the north of Norwich, consented as an NSIP under the Planning Act 2008. A preferred route was published in 2005, the western section across the Wensum Valley was dropped on environmental grounds, the DCO was granted on 2 June 2015, and the road opened fully in April 2018 as the Broadland Northway. Efforts to add a Norwich Western Link to close the ring stalled in 2024-25 after Natural England objected over impacts to rare barbastelle bats.

milestones.title: Preferred route published
milestones.description: Norfolk County Council published its preferred A1067-to-A47 route after the scheme was added to the East of England Regional Spatial Strategy.

milestones.title: Western section dropped
milestones.description: The section west of the A1067 across the Wensum Valley was dropped following environmental concerns.

milestones.title: Removed from East of England Plan
milestones.description: Inspector recommended dropping the NDR from the East of England Plan, citing inadequate consultation.

milestones.title: Programme Entry status awarded
milestones.description: DfT granted Programme Entry status to the eastern A140-to-A47 section, alongside the proposed Rackheath eco-town.

milestones.title: DCO application accepted
milestones.description: PINS accepted the DCO on 4 February 2014.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Preliminary Meeting on 2 June 2014; panel of inspectors appointed.

milestones.title: DCO granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport made the NDR Order 2015 on 2 June 2015.

milestones.title: Construction begins
milestones.description: Main construction began in early 2016 led by Balfour Beatty.

milestones.title: Broadland Northway fully opened
milestones.description: The final section linking the A1151 with the A47 at Postwick opened on 17 April 2018.

milestones.title: Natural England objects to Western Link
milestones.description: Days before NCC's Western Link planning application, Natural England issued new barbastelle bat guidance and objected.

milestones.title: Western Link application withdrawn
milestones.description: NCC withdrew the £274m Western Link planning application after failing to resolve Natural England's bat-mitigation objection.

TR010019.name: M4 Junctions 3 to 12 Smart Motorway
TR010019.summary: A 32-mile (51km) upgrade of the M4 between Hayes (J3) and Theale (J12) to an all-lane running smart motorway, the longest such scheme in England. The DCO was granted in September 2016, construction ran 2018-2022, and the full scheme became operational in December 2022. The project sits within the wider smart motorway safety controversy that led the UK Government to pause new schemes in 2022 and cancel all future smart motorway construction in April 2023.

milestones.title: M4 smart motorway proposed
milestones.description: Government announced intention to upgrade the M4 J3-J12 to a smart motorway; the section carries around 130,000 vehicles per day.

milestones.title: Pre-application work and DCO process begins
milestones.description: Highways England commenced formal DCO pre-application work, including statutory consultation under the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Highways England submitted the application to PINS as an NSIP.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS granted the DCO authorising conversion of the hard shoulder to a permanent running lane.

milestones.title: Construction begins
milestones.description: Balfour Beatty Vinci JV started main construction.

milestones.title: Transport Select Committee inquiry launched
milestones.description: MPs opened an inquiry into smart motorway safety after fatalities rose from 5 in 2017 to 15 in 2019.

milestones.title: Government pauses new smart motorway rollout
milestones.description: Government paused new all-lane running smart motorway schemes pending five years of safety data; M4 was permitted to complete.

milestones.title: Full scheme operational
milestones.description: Eastern section J3-J8/9 opens, completing the 51km all-lane running scheme.

milestones.title: All future smart motorways cancelled
milestones.description: Government announced no new smart motorways would be built, citing cost and lack of public confidence.

TR010023.name: Lake Lothing Third Crossing
TR010023.summary: A third crossing of Lake Lothing in Lowestoft, first proposed in 1918 and revisited periodically through the 1990s and 2000s without action. Suffolk County Council submitted a Development Consent Order in July 2018, consent was granted by the Secretary of State on 30 April 2020, and construction ran from 2021 to 2024. The bridge, named Gull Wing, opened to traffic on 7 September 2024.

milestones.title: First proposal for a third Lowestoft crossing
milestones.description: First suggested just after the First World War, as the town's two existing swing-bridge crossings were already creating congestion.

milestones.title: Suffolk CC unveils third crossing proposals
milestones.description: Designs for a roughly £90m bascule bridge were published, drawing on Lowestoft's offshore wind heritage.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Suffolk CC submitted the Lake Lothing (Lowestoft) Third Crossing DCO; accepted 9 August 2018.

milestones.title: Six-month examination
milestones.description: Hearings and written representations conducted by the Examining Authority.

milestones.title: Development consent granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport made the Lake Lothing (Lowestoft) Third Crossing Order 2020.

milestones.title: Farrans appointed main contractor
milestones.description: Project budget rose from c.£94m to around £146m on revised cost estimates.

milestones.title: Groundbreaking and start of construction
milestones.description: Following preparatory unexploded ordnance surveys; piling underway by mid-2021.

milestones.title: Main bascule span installed
milestones.description: 39.5m bascule span fabricated in the Netherlands, lifted into place over three weeks.

milestones.title: Gull Wing Bridge opens to traffic
milestones.description: 345m bridge opened, providing the third crossing of Lake Lothing.

TR010026.name: A30 Chiverton to Carland Cross Scheme
TR010026.summary: An 8.7-mile upgrade of the last single-carriageway section of the A30 between Camborne and Exeter, dualling between the Chiverton Cross and Carland Cross roundabouts in Cornwall. First proposed in the 1989 Roads for Prosperity white paper but unbuilt for decades, it was revived under the 2014 Road Investment Strategy, granted a DCO in February 2020, and opened to traffic on 24 June 2024.

milestones.title: Included in Roads for Prosperity white paper
milestones.description: Carland Cross to Zelah included in the Conservative government's white paper. Not implemented at the time.

milestones.title: Zelah bypass opens
milestones.description: Construction of the Zelah bypass left the Chiverton-Carland Cross section as the principal remaining single-carriageway pinch point.

milestones.title: Committed in RIS1 (2015-2020)
milestones.description: DfT's first RIS included the dualling among schemes funded for 2015-2020 as part of a £2bn south-west package.

milestones.title: First public consultation on options
milestones.description: Highways England ran the initial non-statutory consultation Oct-Dec 2016.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: Modified version of the consulted option; new dual carriageway north of the existing A30 between Chiverton and Chybucca.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Submitted under section 37 of the Planning Act 2008; accepted for examination 27 September 2018.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: Authorised construction of the 12.5km off-line dual carriageway and grade-separated junctions.

milestones.title: Costain awarded £210m main works
milestones.description: Highways England awarded the main works contract; preliminary works followed.

milestones.title: New dual carriageway opens
milestones.description: Opened several months later than the original March 2024 target due to weather and pandemic delays. Final cost ~£330m.

TR010043.name: Great Yarmouth Third River Crossing
TR010043.summary: A twin-leaf bascule bridge (Herring Bridge) over the River Yare in Great Yarmouth, linking the A47 at Harfrey's Roundabout to South Denes. Following decades of local campaigning and an early consultation in 2009 that stalled for want of funding, the £121m scheme was revived in 2016, secured £98m in the 2017 Budget, received development consent in 2020, and opened to traffic on 1 February 2024.

milestones.title: Initial public consultation
milestones.description: Norfolk CC consulted on four possible bridge/tunnel locations; plans subsequently stalled due to lack of funding.

milestones.title: Made a Norfolk infrastructure priority
milestones.description: NCC formally adopted the Third River Crossing as a strategic priority; ~£1m pledged for further development work.

milestones.title: Stage 2 scheme consultation
milestones.description: Stage 2 in September-October 2017 sought feedback on the developed bridge scheme.

milestones.title: £98m secured in November 2017 Budget
milestones.description: Hammond's Budget allocated £98m of the £120m scheme cost, unlocking the project after years of stalled funding.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: NCC submitted the DCO; accepted for examination in May 2019.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS issued the DCO authorising construction.

milestones.title: Construction starts
milestones.description: BAM Farrans JV began enabling works; demolition of around 35 properties followed Feb-April 2021.

milestones.title: Named 'Herring Bridge'
milestones.description: Following a public vote attracting nearly 6,000 ballots; bascule leaves arrived shortly afterwards.

milestones.title: Bridge opens to traffic
milestones.description: After delays caused by an unexploded WWII bomb (Feb 2023) and a protected water vole burrow (July 2023), the £121m bridge opened.

TR010059.name: A1 in Northumberland - Morpeth to Ellingham
TR010059.summary: Long-promised dualling of c.13 miles of the A1 north of Morpeth, committed in the 2014 Road Investment Strategy at £290m. After consultations from 2016-2019, a single DCO was submitted in July 2020 and examined in 2021. The decision was deferred five times before consent was granted in May 2024, but the scheme was cancelled in the October 2024 Budget on value-for-money grounds and the DCO was formally revoked in November 2025, with c.£70m of sunk costs.

milestones.title: Campaign for A1 dualling begins
milestones.description: Yorkshire section brought up to motorway standard; northbound dualling becomes a long-running North East campaign.

milestones.title: Earlier Morpeth-Felton proposal shelved
milestones.description: Earlier 8-mile Morpeth-Felton scheme not progressed by the interim Regional Transport Board.

milestones.title: RIS 1 commits £290m to dual Morpeth-Ellingham
milestones.description: Cameron and Osborne announce the scheme, intended to deliver continuous dual carriageway from Newcastle to Ellingham.

milestones.title: First public consultation
milestones.description: Highways England ran initial consultation Nov-Dec 2016.

milestones.title: Preferred route announcement
milestones.description: Green option for Morpeth-Felton; Orange option for Alnwick-Ellingham.

milestones.title: Two schemes merged into single DCO
milestones.description: On 30 April 2020 Morpeth-Felton and Alnwick-Ellingham combined into a single DCO; further consultation 16 April-14 May 2020.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: National Highways submitted the combined DCO; accepted for examination on 4 August 2020.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Six-month examination Jan-July 2021; ExA recommendation submitted to SoS on 5 October 2021.

milestones.title: Decision repeatedly deferred
milestones.description: DCO decision postponed five times across 2022-2023 (Jan 2022 → June 2022 → Dec 2022 → Sept 2023 → June 2024).

milestones.title: DCO granted
milestones.description: SoS approved the DCO almost three years after the ExA reported.

milestones.title: Scheme cancelled in Autumn Budget
milestones.description: Chancellor's Budget cancels the scheme citing poor value for money and rising costs estimated above £500m vs the original £290m.

milestones.title: Intent to revoke DCO announced
milestones.description: Transport Secretary signalled formal intent to revoke the 2024 DCO, citing planning blight.

milestones.title: Revocation Order in force
milestones.description: The A1 in Northumberland: Morpeth to Ellingham DC (Revocation) Order 2025 came into force, formally extinguishing the consent. Sunk costs reported at ~£70m.

TR010060.name: A12 Chelmsford to A120 Widening Scheme
TR010060.summary: National Highways scheme to widen ~15 miles of the A12 between J19 (Chelmsford) and J25 (A120) from two to three lanes each way, with junction reconfiguration and two bypasses. Committed in RIS1 (2014), progressed through consultations from 2017, granted a DCO on 12 January 2024 and survived a legal challenge dismissed by the Court of Appeal in August 2024, but cancelled by the Transport Secretary on 8 July 2025 following the Spending Review.

milestones.title: Committed in RIS1
milestones.description: A12 J19-J25 widening included in the first Road Investment Strategy.

milestones.title: Options consultation (4 routes)
milestones.description: Highways England held a public consultation on four route options; 907 responses informed selection of a preferred route.

milestones.title: J23-25 alternative-routes consultation
milestones.description: Additional consultation in October 2019 considered alternative alignments to accommodate the proposed Colchester-Braintree Borders Garden Community.

milestones.title: Preliminary design consultation
milestones.description: Statutory preliminary design consultation including the Rivenhall End bypass.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: National Highways submitted the DCO; accepted for examination 12 September 2022.

milestones.title: Examining Authority recommendation
milestones.description: ExA submitted its recommendation to SoS following the six-month examination.

milestones.title: DCO granted
milestones.description: Mark Harper granted development consent for the £1.3bn scheme.

milestones.title: Court of Appeal dismisses legal challenge
milestones.description: Andrew Boswell's JR (refused permission by HC May 2024) dismissed by the Court of Appeal in August 2024 on greenhouse-gas and cumulative-effects grounds.

milestones.title: Correction Order issued
milestones.description: SoS issued a Correction Order making technical corrections to the DCO.

milestones.title: Scheme cancelled after Spending Review
milestones.description: Heidi Alexander confirmed the scheme would not proceed, citing the Spending Review; Essex CC and local MPs criticised the decision.

TR010064.name: M60/M62/M66 Simister Island
TR010064.summary: National Highways scheme to upgrade the M60 Junction 18 (Simister Island) interchange where the M60, M62 and M66 meet north of Manchester, adding free-flow links (the 'Northern Loop' including the Pike Fold Viaduct). The DCO was granted on 9 September 2025; construction is expected to begin after March 2026 subject to RIS3 funding, taking around 2-4 years.

milestones.title: Upgrade need identified
milestones.description: Highways England's Route Based Strategy flagged Simister Island (originally opened 1971 as a two-level roundabout, with a 1999 capacity upgrade) as a pinch point requiring further intervention.

milestones.title: Committed in RIS2
milestones.description: Government's RIS2 (2020-2025) committed funding to upgrade the junction.

milestones.title: Options consultation
milestones.description: Two route options consulted on; Northern Loop supported by 67% vs 24% for Inner Links.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: Northern Loop selected; design includes the Pike Fold Viaduct.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: National Highways submitted the DCO; accepted for examination on 30 April 2024.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Six-month examination Sep 2024 - Mar 2025.

milestones.title: DCO granted
milestones.description: Heidi Alexander granted the DCO for the Northern Loop scheme (estimated £207-£340m, Costain-led).

milestones.title: Construction expected to begin
milestones.description: Build phase anticipated to start after March 2026 once RIS3 funding is confirmed.

milestones.title: Projected opening
milestones.description: Scheme expected to open to traffic around 2028-2029.

TR010016.name: A63 Castle Street Improvement-Hull
TR010016.summary: Grade-separated underpass and active-travel scheme on the A63 through central Hull, first proposed in the 1980s and consented in 2020 after the Secretary of State overruled the Examining Authority's recommendation to refuse. Construction ran 2020-2026 — including the largest scientific excavation of a post-medieval burial ground in Northern England — with the Mytongate underpass opening to traffic on 23 March 2026.

milestones.title: Castle Street improvement first proposed
milestones.description: Improvement schemes for the A63 through Hull city centre planned from the 1980s but repeatedly deferred for funding.

milestones.title: Highways Agency design consultation
milestones.description: First public consultation on options for grade-separating the A63 at Mytongate.

milestones.title: Preferred scheme announced
milestones.description: Highways Agency selected lowering the A63 by 7m at Mytongate, with Ferensway/Commercial Road raised by 1m.

milestones.title: Second public consultation
milestones.description: Government announced funding for a c.£160m scheme post-2015.

milestones.title: Balfour Beatty awarded ECI contract
milestones.description: £75m early contractor involvement contract for design development and delivery.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Highways England submitted the DCO; accepted for examination on 18 October 2018.

milestones.title: Examination hearings begin
milestones.description: Issues including Trinity Burial Ground, the Grade II listed Earl de Grey pub and pedestrian severance.

milestones.title: DCO granted by Secretary of State
milestones.description: SoS for Transport overruled the Examining Authority's recommendation to refuse, concluding the public benefits outweighed the harms.

milestones.title: Main construction starts
milestones.description: Balfour Beatty began on-site works on the £355m scheme; included excavation of c.10,000 burials at Trinity Burial Ground.

milestones.title: Murdoch's Connection footbridge opens
milestones.description: 47m-tall pedestrian and cycle bridge over the A63 linking the city centre to the marina.

milestones.title: Porter Street footbridge opens
milestones.description: Second active-travel bridge over the A63, west of Mytongate.

milestones.title: Completion delayed by a year
milestones.description: National Highways announced a c.12-month delay due to challenging ground conditions near the Humber.

milestones.title: Mytongate underpass opens to traffic
milestones.description: The 400m grade-separated A63 underpass opened; final landscaping continuing into spring 2026.

TR010037.name: A47 - A11 Thickthorn Junction
TR010037.summary: National Highways scheme to remodel the A47/A11 Thickthorn interchange south of Norwich with two free-flow underpass slip roads, one of four parallel A47 corridor NSIPs under RIS1/RIS2. DCO granted October 2022; survived the Andrew Boswell judicial review on cumulative climate grounds at the High Court (July 2023), Court of Appeal (Feb 2024) and Supreme Court (May 2024) — a leading authority on the limits of cumulative carbon assessment.
